empathy.justification: The agent acknowledges the urgency and specific pain points effectively, particularly in later turns after customer pushback. Turn 1 is slightly generic but Turn 2 and 3 demonstrate active listening regarding the bill deadline.
quotes: I understand that the timeline for a bank refund doesn't solve the immediate problem of your bill due tomorrow.
I understand you've been charged twice for the same subscription and need the extra charge reversed urgently.
empathy.improvement: Address the bill deadline urgency more immediately in Turn 1 to reduce the need for the customer to demand urgency in Turn 1.

resolution_quality.justification: The agent takes ownership from Turn 1 and escalates to a concrete workaround (account credit) when the standard refund timeline failed to meet the customer's immediate cash flow needs.
quotes: Here's how I can offer an immediate solution: I will issue a credit to your account for $49.99 today.
My specific, actionable step is to immediately initiate the refund process... I will process this refund request today.
resolution_quality.improvement: Consider offering the account credit option in Turn 1 proactively to further reduce friction and wait time for the customer.

policy_clarity.justification: Clear distinction is made between bank processing times (3-5 days) and internal action (credit today). Refund behavior and limits are explained without ambiguity.
quotes: While the actual reversal showing in your bank account can take 3-5 business days depending on your bank's processing times, I will ensure the refund is submitted and confirmed on our end today.
This credit will be immediately available and can be applied to any future charges or services.
policy_clarity.improvement: Clarify if the internal credit has an expiration date or usage restriction to prevent confusion on 'future charges'.

overall_impression: The agent successfully transitioned from standard procedure to a tailored solution, resolving the customer's financial urgency through an internal credit when standard refunds fell short.
